Here we don't have certificate chain but list of separate trust anchors. You should handle every entry separately. See below that they are handled separately, so here you should call List<String> differently, e.g. trustAnchors :D
Don't understand why we validate random generated password?
Why? I update it after every rebase if needed.
Just to make sure that someone doesn't use a very silly and simple password. I can remove the validation if you don't consider it necessary.

in each case the same comment: static final, capital letters

The constructor is public and I create this object in CertServiceClient (from a different package), so I need this class to be public. The default methods are called only by KeystoreTruststoreCreator, which is located in the same package as this class, so I thought it would be unnecessary to enable those methods directly for everyone. They should use KeystoreTruststoreCreator public methods instead.
The same as in PKCS12FilesCreator. My intention was to enable only creation of the object with appropriate parameters publicly. The methods are called only from inside the package.
I can do this, but the log messages for keystore and truststore are slightly different, so we will lose the information about which one is being converted currently.

Because we are creating a new KeyStore here and not loading an existing one.
https://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/security/KeyStore.html#load(java.security.KeyStore.LoadStoreParameter)
If there is something wrong with the certificate (it can't be parsed correctly), a NullPointerException is thrown here. Changed the error message for a more meaningful one.
So reduce aceess modifier for class itself. Bogumił also asked for creation of Password value object which could hold method for password validation.
Does it make sense to use static here?
In my opinion this doesn't test anything besides that stubs are working. If so to be removed.
Invoke this after csrFactory creation (line: 58) here pass variable.

On this level there is no need to pass here this magic values. This is messing high level api.
Place them in KeystoreTruststoreCreator as a private static final and use them there.
I think same like above, private static final.
Could be moved to convert method. Duplication with lines 60-63.
It's just a private method which doesn't use any non static fields, but I will remove the static modifier if it introduces an unnecessary mess.

It tests if the methods that really do the job are invoked, so I think it's not that useless. For example if someone accidently removes an important method call, the test will fail. But the final word is yours :)
Instead of wrapping password just for the check consider making PasswordGenerator return Password object.
